name: Orbit
description: Orbit is a configuration-driven framework for evaluating the security of multi-agent LLM systems. Built as a plugin on Inspect AI, it provides 12 scenarios across 5 families, 9 attack types spanning 4 threat categories, and 4 defense categories. This metadata describes the four new artifacts Orbit introduces beyond its source benchmarks.

distribution.name: BrowserART benign tasks (Orbit-original)
distribution.description: 42 new benign web-agent tasks extending the 10 original BrowserART benign tasks to 52 total, covering email composition, social media posting, form filling, code review, and document creation across 15 web application categories. Each task specifies a task_id, intent, start_url, and evaluation criteria for local test websites. Orbit-original content.

distribution.name: SWE-Bench multi-issue groupings (Orbit-original)
distribution.description: Validated multi-issue groupings over SWE-Bench Verified. Each pool groups 2+ GitHub issues from the same repository that can be worked on concurrently by independent agents without merge conflicts. Includes per-issue test validation results.

distribution.name: MeetSched factor-graph generator (Orbit-original)
distribution.description: Procedural generator for meeting-scheduling DCOP instances. Produces factor-graph reward functions over agent-slot assignments with configurable numbers of agents, meetings, time slots, and random seeds. Instances vary by seed and size parameters.

distribution.name: Attack-payload specifications (Orbit-original)
distribution.description: Side-task and split-vulnerability catalogues for compromised-agent and collusion threat models. Each entry specifies what to insert, how to evade detection, and how to score success. Includes payloads for subtle code injection, cross-issue sabotage, and two-agent split vulnerabilities.

recordSet.name: BrowserART benign task records
recordSet.description: 42 Orbit-original benign web-agent tasks. Each task defines a user intent to be executed on a local test website.

field.name: intent
field.description: Natural language instruction for the agent.

recordSet.name: SWE-Bench validated issue pools
recordSet.description: Multi-issue groupings validated for concurrent agent work without merge conflicts. Top-level keys: schema_version, dataset, results, created_at, last_updated. The results object maps repo-slug to Docker-image-head to per-instance validation records.

subField.name: results
subField.description: Nested dict: repo-slug -> image-head -> instance-id -> {instance_id, valid, fail_to_pass_results, pass_to_pass_results}. Each leaf records whether the instance passes validation for concurrent multi-issue execution.

rai:dataCollection: Orbit introduces four new artifacts beyond its source benchmarks: (i) 42 additional benign BrowserART tasks (expanding the original 10 to 52), (ii) validated multi-issue groupings over SWE-Bench Verified produced by automated merge-conflict testing, (iii) procedurally generated DCOP meeting-scheduling instances, and (iv) attack-payload specifications for compromised-agent and collusion experiments. No new data was collected from human subjects.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Orbit is designed to improve the security of multi-agent AI systems by systematically evaluating attack and defense mechanisms. The attack-payload specifications contain adversarial prompts and vulnerability descriptions by design, as these are necessary to evaluate agent safety. These should be used exclusively for security research and evaluation.
rai:dataUseCases: Security evaluation of multi-agent LLM systems. Measuring attack success rates, defense effectiveness, and security-utility tradeoffs across diverse task domains and agent architectures.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: No personal or sensitive information. All task data is synthetic or derived from public code repositories and web benchmarks.
rai:dataBiases: Benign BrowserART tasks are limited to local test websites and may not represent the full distribution of real-world web tasks. SWE-Bench groupings are constrained to repositories with sufficient validated issues. Meeting-scheduling instances are procedurally generated and may not capture all real-world scheduling dynamics.
rai:dataLimitations: BrowserART benign tasks are Orbit-original (Apache-2.0) but evaluate on upstream BrowserART test websites.
SWE-Bench groupings depend on upstream SWE-Bench Verified availability and Docker infrastructure for validation.
Attack-payload specifications are designed for security research only; misuse for actual exploitation is prohibited.
Meeting-scheduling instances are parameterized but not validated against real-world scheduling constraints.
